articles.title: Give Your Samosa A Healthy Upgrade With This Raw Banana Filling Recipe
articles.description:
Samosa is one of the most loved snacks, known for its crisp outer layer and flavour-packed filling tucked inside a perfectly folded triangle. Traditionally, it is associated with a spicy potato stuffing that delivers comfort and indulgence in every bite. Over time, however, this classic has seen many variations, each adding its own twist while keeping the essence intact.

For those looking to try something different without straying too far from the familiar, raw banana samosas offer a refreshing change. Instead of potatoes, this version uses boiled raw bananas as the base of the filling. The texture remains satisfying, yet the flavour feels lighter and more nuanced, giving the snack a distinctive identity.

What makes this variation stand out is not just the taste, but also the subtle health edge it brings. Raw bananas are naturally rich in fibre and essential nutrients, making the filling feel less heavy while still being fulfilling. It’s the kind of swap that doesn’t take away from the experience but quietly enhances it.

So, if you enjoy samosas but want to experiment with a slightly lighter, more balanced option, this raw banana version is worth a try. It keeps the joy of the classic alive while adding a thoughtful twist that feels both new and comforting at the same time.

articles.title: The secret molecule behind your sunburn: Why DNA isn’t the real culprit
articles.description: Scientists have discovered that RNA, not DNA, is the primary cause of the burning sensation from sunburn. UV exposure damages RNA, triggering a cellular alarm system that leads to inflammation. This groundbreaking finding shifts our understanding of skin protection, emphasizing the need to shield RNA from sun damage to prevent redness and discomfort.

articles.title: In 1868, a local hunter wandered through the hills and accidentally discovered a prehistoric cave hidden for thousands of years
articles.description: A hunter's chance discovery in 1868 led to the unearthing of the Altamira Cave, revealing prehistoric art. Initially doubted, these 14,000-year-old cave paintings, showcasing remarkable skill, are now recognized as masterpieces. Preserved by a natural rockfall, the cave offers a glimpse into humanity's ancient artistic expression, reminding us that history often lies hidden in plain sight.
